subject: Sociology
title: Globalization and Risk
description: A short lesson to help you apply Globalization and Risk.

hook.text: A virus in Wuhan shut down the entire planet. Globalization connected us — and made us all vulnerable.

buildup.text: Ulrich Beck described the risk society: modern life creates global risks that no single nation can control.

discovery.text: Climate change, pandemics, nuclear threats, and financial crises are manufactured risks with global reach.

twist.text: Risks are democratic in reach but not in impact. The poor suffer most from crises they didn't create.

climax.text: Globalization binds humanity together in shared risk. Solving these threats requires cooperation beyond borders.

quiz.question: What is Beck's 'risk society'?
options: Modern life creates global risks no single nation can control
